Week of 1/26 - 1/30

Weekly Market Summary

By Noah Halfon

Global Weekly Market Movements

U.S. Equities

  • S&P 500 Index: 6,939.03 (0.23%)
    • SPX ends the week slightly positive and closes out January green among new Fed elections and big tech earnings. XLE energy was the largest gainer of the week, up 3.27% as natural gas prices rose due to winter storms, while XLC communication services followed closely at 3.05%. XLV healthcare suffered the most this week falling -2.82% following revised medicare rates.
  • NASDAQ Composite: 23,461.82 (-0.29%)
  • Dow Jones Industrial Average: 48,892.47 (-0.50%)
    • Dow closes out the week negative but remains positive for the month ended. This is the ninth consecutive month of gains for the Dow.
  • Russell 3000 Index: 3,939.91 (-0.13%)

Big Movers of the Week

    • Sandisk Corp (SNDK): 576.25 (21.06%)
      • Sandisk soars after reporting blockbuster Q4 earnings. Beating EPS expectations by 75% coming in at 6.20 vs. $3.62 and beating revenue by 12% (+64% YoY), the memory company continues to gain after being the best performing name in the S&P 500 last year. Memory demand continues to grow strongly but remains at a premium as demand for AI chips only grows.
    • United Health Group (UNH): 286.93 (-18.90%)
      • UNH lost almost 20% on Tuesday following earnings and reports that White House proposed keeping medicare advantage rates flat this year. Expectations for Medicare rates were a 4-5% raise, meaning lower rates translate into lower earnings as a large amount of UNH’s business is generated from medicare. UNH earnings came through hitting EPS targets, but missing on revenue, as well as citing lower FY 2026 revenue expectations. This is the first annual drop in decades for UNH. The healthcare sector across the board lost $100B in market cap on Tuesday.
    • Microsoft (MSFT): 430.29 (-7.48%)
      • MSFT fell 10% after earnings with its worst day in 5 years. Over $400B in market cap was wiped out after the tech company reported Azure growth slowing to 39%, capex increasing 66% to $37.5B, and gross margins hitting a three-year low. OpenAI also accounts for 45% of Microsoft's $625B backlog, showing concerns over concentrations and development where Microsoft appears to be falling behind competitors.

Chinese Equities - Shanghai Composite (SHCOMP): 4,117.95 (-0.65%)

Hong Kong Equities - Hang Seng Index (HSI): 27,387.11 (2.02%)

Japanese Equities- Nikkei 225 (NI225): 53,322.85 (0.56%)

European Equities

  • UK Index (UKX): 10,223.54 (0.74%)
  • German Index (DAX): 24,538.81 (-1.41%)

Commodities

  • Gold Futures: 4,907.50 (-3.87%)

  • Silver Futures: 85.25 (-20.85%)
    • Precious metals fall after Trump appoints Kevin Warsh to the next Federal Reserve chair. Known for his supportive stance on tighter liquidity conditions, investors see this as less liquidity in the system and long term strength in the USD, diminishing the debasement trade that helped push gold and silver to new ATHs.
  • Crude Oil Futures: 65.74 (7.44%)

Crypto

  • Bitcoin (BTC): 81,075.16 (-8.14%)
  • Ethereum (ETH): 2,525.49 (-13.71%)
    • Crypto selloff follows similar rationale as gold and silver. Concerns over liquidity tightening and continued wiping out of leveraged positions as alternative stores of value become less attractive under investors' perceptions of Kevin Warsh.

U.S. Monetary & Fiscal Policy

FOMC + Warsh: The Federal Reserve holds rates unchanged at 3.5%-3.75% citing a stabilizing labor market and inflation still above the target rate of 2%. Powell also said he expects tariff price pressures to fade by mid 2026. Following this week's FOMC meeting, Trump confirmed Kevin Warsh as his nominee for the next Fed chair. Warsh is a known hawk, critical of loose monetary policy and has frequently commented that inflation should be the primary concern for the Fed. The nomination triggered a selloff in markets, as investors sow fears of a restrictive balance sheet and concerns over crisis management. This news sent the dollar surging more than 50 bps as Warsh may be more focused on price pressures and monetary support for the dollar. Precious metals sold off; more on metals above.

Global Macroeconomic News

Trade: The EU and India finalized a landmark free trade agreement after nearly two decades of stalled negotiations. Commission President Ursula von der Leyen called it "the mother of all deals," as the pact eliminates tariffs on 96.6% of EU exports to India and 99.5% of Indian goods entering Europe over seven years, with EU exports to India expected to double by 2032. Also regarding Canada, Trump threatened 100% tariffs against all Canadian exports to America if it makes a trade deal with China.

Consumer Confidence: US consumer confidence falls to 84.5 in January reaching its lowest level in 12 years. Survey results indicate that consumers are more worried than ever about inflation and a weakening labor market. The highest number of consumers also commented on it being harder to get jobs since February 2021 post pandemic.

//graph showing consumer confidence in labor market highest

Banco de la República: Colombia raised its benchmark interest rate by a full percentage point to 10.25%, more than any analyst had expected, as a record minimum wage increase ramps up inflation pressures. The central bank flagged higher inflation expectations in January and strong Q4 2025 economic activity, signaling this could be the start of a new tightening cycle.

Sources: Google Finance, Market Watch, CME Fedwatch, Yahoo Finance, Reuters, New York Times, Bloomberg, Wall St Journal, Washington Post, US Department of the Treasury, GuruFocus, Edward Jones

Noah Halfon
